skill.name: react-dev
skill.description: Guides type-safe React development with TypeScript, React 18-19 patterns, Server Components, hooks, events, generic components, and router integration.
skill.summary: Build type-safe React components, hooks, events, server components, and routes with TypeScript patterns.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, credential, network, filesystem, and keylogger indicators, but review found Markdown documentation and TypeScript examples rather than executable skill code. The remaining risk is low: examples include legitimate React fetch, localStorage, dynamic import, and server-only environment access patterns that should be used with normal application security review.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer classified many Markdown backticks and code fences as Ruby or shell execution. Review found TypeScript, TSX, JSON, and bash documentation examples, with no executable scripts or automated command execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown examples and inline technical terms, not code executed by the skill. No package scripts, hooks, or runtime files were found in the file list.

low_findings.title: React Key Terminology Misclassified as Sensitive Material
low_findings.description: Several high findings are caused by React key props, keyboard event names, route type paths, and password field examples. These are normal React TypeScript documentation patterns, not certificate files or leaked secret keys.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context shows UI key props, keyboard handling, generated route type paths, and form validation examples. No private key block, credential file, or embedded secret value was found.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Example Network and Storage APIs
low_findings.description: The skill includes React examples using fetch, localStorage, dynamic import, and process.env.SECRET_KEY. These are documentation samples for client hooks and server-only React code, not evidence of exfiltration or hidden runtime behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The patterns are real API examples, so they should be reviewed when copied into applications. The surrounding text frames them as React documentation and no unauthorized endpoint or credential transmission is shown.

low_findings.title: Keylogger Heuristic False Positive
low_findings.description: The critical keylogger keyword hit is a React event handling example that logs form input and keyboard events for teaching event types. It does not install listeners globally, persist data, or transmit captured keystrokes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code is local component event handler documentation. No persistence, background collection, network send, or prompt text instructing surveillance behavior was found.

content.value_statement: React TypeScript work often fails because component, hook, event, and router types are inconsistent. This skill provides focused patterns for React 18-19 so Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can produce safer typed UI code.
actual_capabilities: Provides TypeScript patterns for React component props, children, refs, and discriminated unions.
Shows typed event handler patterns for forms, inputs, keyboard, mouse, focus, drag, and clipboard events.
Explains hook typing for useState, useRef, useReducer, useContext, custom hooks, and tuple returns.
Covers React 19 patterns including ref as prop, useActionState, use(), optimistic updates, and Server Components.
Includes router guidance for TanStack Router and React Router with typed params, loaders, actions, and route modules.
Documents reusable generic components such as tables, lists, selects, forms, and data display helpers.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not run tests or validate generated React code by itself.
It focuses on web React with TypeScript, not React Native or vanilla JavaScript React projects.
Some examples require framework support such as React 19, server actions, or router-specific type generation.
Security, accessibility, and performance still require project-specific review before production release.

use_cases.title: Create Typed UI Components
use_cases.description: Build reusable buttons, forms, lists, tables, and modal components with precise TypeScript props and native element support.

use_cases.title: Migrate React 18 Code
use_cases.description: Update components and forms for React 19 patterns, including ref as prop, useActionState, Server Components, and use().

use_cases.title: Standardize Router Types
use_cases.description: Apply typed route params, loaders, actions, search params, and route modules in TanStack Router or React Router projects.

prompt_templates.title: Type a Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a typed React Button component in TypeScript. Extend native button props and add a variant prop.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Event Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React form component and replace broad event types with precise TypeScript event types.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Generic Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a generic typed table component with typed columns, custom cell rendering, sorting callbacks, and safe key extraction.

prompt_templates.title: Design React 19 Data Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a React 19 page using Server Components, a Server Action, optimistic UI, and typed router params. Explain the type boundaries.

output_examples.input: Build a typed search form with submit and change handlers.
output_examples.output: Returns a React TypeScript form design with precise form and input event types, typed state, validation notes, and accessibility-friendly attributes.

output_examples.input: Create a reusable table for user records.
output_examples.output: Produces a generic component plan with typed columns, row keys, custom render functions, sorting callbacks, and example usage guidance.

output_examples.input: Migrate a component library to React 19 refs.
output_examples.output: Explains how to replace forwardRef patterns with ref-as-prop signatures and highlights compatibility checks for older React versions.

best_practices: Share the target React version, router choice, and TypeScript strictness before asking for implementation help.
Ask for native element prop extension when building reusable components that wrap HTML elements.
Request a short explanation of type boundaries when mixing Server Components, Client Components, and Server Actions.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad any-based props when the component has known states or variants.
Do not copy server-only environment examples into client components or browser bundles.
Do not mix React Router and TanStack Router patterns in the same request unless migration is the goal.

faq.question: Does this skill write React code?
faq.answer: It guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to write typed React examples and explain TypeScript patterns.

faq.question: Which React versions does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers React 18 and React 19, with specific notes for React 19 changes and Server Components.

faq.question: Can it help with React Router?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes React Router v7 guidance for typed route modules, loaders, actions, and generated route types.

faq.question: Can it help with TanStack Router?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers typed params, search validation, loaders, navigation, and route context patterns.

faq.question: Is it suitable for React Native?
faq.answer: No. The skill is focused on web React patterns and browser event types.

faq.question: Does it replace project testing?
faq.answer: No. Generated code should still be checked with TypeScript, tests, linting, accessibility review, and security review.
